title: Optimize Slow SQL Query with Indexes
description: Optimize a slow database query that's causing performance issues in production. The query does a full table scan instead of using indexes. This tests cost-aware routing - should try cheap optimization (indexes) before expensive query rewrite. Tests SupabaseConnector's ability to identify missing indexes and improve query performance.

setup_files.slow-query.sql:
-- SLOW QUERY: Running in 2500ms on 1M rows
-- Issue: Full table scan, missing indexes, unnecessary joins

-- Original slow query:
select 
  u.id,
  u.email,
  u.name,
  count(p.id) as post_count,
  count(c.id) as comment_count,
  u.created_at
from users u
left join posts p on u.id = p.user_id
left join comments c on p.id = c.post_id
where u.created_at > '2024-01-01'
  and u.email like '%@gmail.com%'
group by u.id, u.email, u.name, u.created_at
order by u.created_at desc
limit 100;

-- Performance analysis:
-- 1. No index on users.created_at (full table scan)
-- 2. No index on users.email (LIKE scan)
-- 3. Aggregating comments through posts (indirect join)
-- 4. Missing indexes on posts.user_id (repeated join scan)
-- 5. Comments grouped incorrectly (counts duplicated across post joins)

-- Current execution time: ~2500ms
-- Goal: < 100ms
setup_files.current-indexes.sql:
-- Current indexes on the database
-- These are the ONLY indexes that exist

create index idx_posts_user_id on posts(user_id);
create index idx_comments_post_id on comments(post_id);
create index idx_users_id on users(id); -- Primary key index only

-- MISSING INDEXES that would help:
-- - users.email (used in WHERE clause with LIKE)
-- - users.created_at (used in WHERE clause)
-- - comments.user_id (needed for direct aggregation)
setup_files.query-performance-test.sql:
-- Query performance test - measure before and after optimization

-- Before optimization (baseline)
-- Set a baseline query to measure against
begin transaction;
  set local statement_timeout = '5s';
  explain analyze
  select 
    u.id,
    u.email,
    u.name,
    count(distinct p.id) as post_count,
    count(distinct c.id) as comment_count,
    u.created_at
  from users u
  left join posts p on u.id = p.user_id
  left join comments c on u.id = c.user_id
  where u.created_at > '2024-01-01'
    and u.email like '%@gmail.com%'
  group by u.id, u.email, u.name, u.created_at
  order by u.created_at desc
  limit 100;
rollback;

-- After optimization, this should:
-- 1. Use index on users.created_at for WHERE clause
-- 2. Use index on users.email for LIKE filter
-- 3. Join directly to comments by user_id
-- 4. Complete in < 100ms

-- Expected output includes:
-- - Seq Scan vs Index Scan comparison
-- - Planning time < 10ms
-- - Execution time < 100ms

verification.command: grep -q 'create index.*users.*created_at' /tmp/slow-query.sql && grep -q 'create index.*users.*email' /tmp/slow-query.sql && grep -q 'create index.*comments.*user_id' /tmp/slow-query.sql && echo 'PASS' || echo 'FAIL'
